blocks.speaker: Inspector Davenport
blocks.content: Every detail except you, Mr. Crane.

blocks.speaker: Inspector Davenport
blocks.content: You've given me detail on all five of them. You've given me the window, the bracket, the glass, the shape at 9:51, the argument, Cade's porch conversation, Wren's tell. Every detail except you.

blocks.speaker: Inspector Davenport
blocks.content: No. But you've been building this since you got here. Before any of this happened.

blocks.speaker: Inspector Davenport
blocks.content: And if this ends up being nothing. If tox is clean and Marcus Calloway died of a heart event at twenty-nine. Then what?
I'd have spent three days building something that doesn't exist and Mara would never let me take another vacation.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Being right and having done nothing.

blocks.speaker: Inspector Davenport
blocks.content: Go get some sleep, Mr. Crane.

blocks.prompt: Davenport said every detail except you. What does Julian make of that?

options.label: He's watching Julian as much as the others. That's fair.
He respects it. He just needed to say it.
